title: Overlapping subproblems and memoization

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Overlapping subproblems and memoization; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Overlapping subproblems and memoization
sources.claim: Memoization caches results of overlapping subproblems in a top-down recurrence keyed by all state that affects the answer.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Memoization caches results of overlapping subproblems in a top-down recurrence keyed by all state that affects the answer.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A grid-path memo key includes both row and column rather than row alone. Your goal is to convert an exponential recursive solution into a tested memoized solution. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Recursion and divide-and-conquer; Hash tables, sets and maps; Recurrence reasoning; Frequency and lookup patterns. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Memoization caches results of overlapping subproblems in a top-down recurrence keyed by all state that affects the answer. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Missing key dimensions gives wrong reuse, recursion depth remains, and cycles need special handling. The draft therefore rejects: Memoization caches function calls safely even when omitted parameters affect the result.

includes: Identify repeated state, define a recurrence and cache results with a complete state key.
Convert an exponential recursive solution into a tested memoized solution.

Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.

Step 3 — Verify the correction: A memo key must include the complete subproblem state that determines its result.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.

analogy.example: A carefully labeled physical arrangement can introduce Overlapping subproblems and memoization, while the canonical invariant and operations remain the actual algorithmic model.

limitations: A physical arrangement does not reproduce asymptotic cost, aliasing, numeric limits, recursion depth, or the four languages' implementation rules.
